name: Kauai Ohana
description_short: Kauai Ohana Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union serving Hawaii with auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and comprehensive banking services.
description_long:
Kauai Ohana Federal Credit Union (KOFCU) is a federally chartered, not-for-profit credit union operating in Hawaii and serving members through both physical locations and digital channels. The organization is insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), which provides the same deposit protection as FDIC insurance for bank accounts. The credit union maintains a website presence at kauaiohanafcu.org and can be reached at (808) 245-3753 for member inquiries.

KOFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (as low as 3.50-4.00% APR), personal loans (as low as 4.75-5.25% APR), mortgage and HELOC loans, lines of credit (as low as 6.00% APR), and savings products including 6-month CDs (3.815% APY). Members have access to free online banking with bill payment functionality, free mobile banking through the Kauai Teachers FCU Mobile App, Mastercard debit cards with the SecureCard control feature, direct deposit, e-statements, overdraft protection, notary services, and surcharge-free ATM access nationwide. The credit union emphasizes account management and debit card control through mobile and online platforms.

Kauai Ohana FCU distinguishes itself as a member-owned financial cooperative rather than a for-profit bank, meaning profits are returned to members through better rates and lower fees. The institution provides localized service with Hawaii-based phone support and appears to serve a community-focused membership base. Their SecureCard debit card feature allows granular control over when, where, and how cards are used, reflecting modern fraud prevention priorities. The credit union recently launched a redesigned website, indicating ongoing digital modernization.

As a credit union, KOFCU is best suited for individuals who value member-ownership and community banking over corporate banking chains. However, prospective members should note that credit unions typically have membership eligibility requirements (geographic, employment, or associational) that may restrict who can join. The website does not clearly display membership eligibility criteria, membership fees, or minimum deposit requirements, which are important considerations for potential members. Loan rates shown are promotional minimums and actual rates depend on creditworthiness.
services: Free online banking with fund transfers
Mastercard debit cards with SecureCard control
pros: Auto loans available as low as 3.50% APR, competitive for credit union financing
Free nationwide surcharge-free ATM network access for members
Free online banking and bill payment services included with membership
SecureCard debit card feature enables detailed transaction controls and fraud prevention
NCUA-insured deposits provide same protection level as FDIC-insured bank accounts
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits returned to members vs. shareholders
Mobile app available on both iOS and Android platforms for account management
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not disclosed on website—credit unions restrict who can join
No clear information about membership fees, account minimums, or opening requirements
Limited transparency on mortgage rates (listed as 'call for rates' only) vs. transparent rate quotes
Personal loan rates start at 4.75% but actual approval rates depend on credit and are not pre-qualifying
Website does not specify service hours, branch locations, or physical accessibility information
best_for: Hawaii residents or employees eligible for KOFCU membership seeking competitive auto and personal loan rates
Individuals wanting member-owned financial institution with community focus over corporate banking
People prioritizing debit card security and transaction-level control features
Members seeking comprehensive digital banking with free online and mobile services
diagnosis: Kauai Ohana FCU is best for Hawaii-based individuals who qualify for membership and want competitive loan rates with member-ownership benefits. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is not transparently disclosed on their website—potential members must contact the credit union directly to confirm they meet eligibility requirements (typically geographic, employment, or family-based criteria), and specific account minimums and fees are not clearly stated online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Kauai Ohana — Credit Union in Lihue, HI | CreditDoc
meta_description: Kauai Ohana — NCUA-insured credit union in Lihue, HI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
